title: Phase 32.4B — Sitemap Intent Audit + Discovery Proof Gate: Final Report

key_findings.phase_32_4_correction: Phase 32.4's claim of '353 noindexed tools + guides creating 53.8% sitemap bloat' was WRONG. The sitemap filter (isFormulaVariant) correctly excludes noindexed formula variants. The 225 tools and 125 guides in the sitemap are INDEXABLE (index,follow, self-canonical, HTTP 200). Phase 31.1's finding of 'no noindex URLs in sitemap' was CORRECT.
key_findings.sitemap_technical_health: EXCELLENT — 97.5% clean. Only 3 polluted URLs (1 404 + 2 noindex contradictions). All other 653 URLs are HTTP 200, indexable, self-canonical.
key_findings.content_bloat: MODERATE — 350 thin tool/guide pages are technically valid but low-value. They dilute sitemap quality and contribute to the 503 discovered-not-indexed count.
key_findings.six_url_discovery: ON_TRACK for Day 0 — 3 of 6 discovered within hours. 0 crawled, 0 indexed. Too early to evaluate.
key_findings.dni_stability: STABLE — 503 discovered-not-indexed, unchanged from previous measurement.

verdicts.sitemap_composition: CLEAN but BLOATED — technically pristine, content-wise inflated
verdicts.indexation_intent: NO_PARTIAL_BLOAT_FOUND — 350 thin pages exist but are indexable

on_track_off_track: ON_TRACK — Sitemap is technically clean. 3 polluted URLs are fixable. Phase 32 URLs are progressing through discovery (3/6 in 24 hours). The only blocker is time — pages need 3-7 days for Google crawling.

next_recommended_actions.action: Fix 3 polluted sitemap URLs: remove 1 404 + 2 noindex contradictions
Day 3 GSC check (Jun 21) — verify 6 URL crawl/indexing progress
Day 7 GSC check (Jun 25) — full scale readiness reassessment
Phase 32.5 decision: prune 125 guides + 66 thin tools from sitemap? Or keep for now?

success_condition: MET — Phase 32.4B determined that the 656-URL sitemap is TECHNICALLY SAFE (97.5% clean) but CONTENT-BLOATED (350 thin pages). The 6 Phase 32 URLs are progressing through discovery (3/6 discovered) without worsening DNI. The sitemap is NOT dangerous — it's clean but inflated.
